title: Mechanical Engineering Workflow Assurance AI Management System

sources.title: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 official public record
sources.notes: Public description used for high-level alignment only; no conformity or certification claim.

sources.title: ISO/IEC 23894:2023 official public record
sources.notes: Public abstract used to confirm the AI-specific risk-management purpose.

sources.title: NIST AI Risk Management Framework 1.0
sources.notes: Voluntary public framework used for Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage practices. NIST states that version 1.0 is being updated; publication of a revision triggers governance review.

sources.title: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile
sources.notes: Public generative-AI risk guidance used for project-assistant controls.

included: Generative-AI assistance used for project planning, research, coding, testing, documentation, and repository operations.
The deterministic package-assurance evaluator as an AI-adjacent control component.
Future model-based capabilities while they are proposed, assessed, or blocked.
People, data, software, suppliers, evidence, and decisions involved in those lifecycles.
excluded: Autonomous engineering sign-off or safety-critical final decisions.
Production hosting, API, database, RAG, generic agent, PDF/CAD extraction, and model-training capabilities not approved for v0.3.0.

needs: Accurate status, bounded claims, explicit risk, and final release authority.
Traceable evidence, uncertainty, limitations, and no substitution for engineering judgment.
Reliable findings, protected information, contestability, and understandable release holds.

interested_parties.party: People affected by engineering package decisions
needs: No autonomous unsafe decision, preserved accountability, and qualified review.
Authorized use, privacy, confidentiality, attribution, and license compliance.

roles.name: Project owner
accountabilities: Approve policy, material scope, high-risk acceptance, and release.
Resolve non-waivable holds through recorded decisions.
roles.independence_rule: Cannot describe self-review as independent assurance.

roles.name: AI management representative and project manager
accountabilities: Maintain inventory, risks, controls, objectives, audits, and management reviews.
Escalate unresolved risk and verify corrective-action evidence.
roles.independence_rule: Must obtain owner approval for high-risk acceptance and release.

roles.name: Implementer or producing agent
accountabilities: Implement only authorized work and preserve inputs, outputs, traces, and failures.
Run focused and regression checks and disclose limitations.
roles.independence_rule: Cannot independently accept its own high-risk implementation or benchmark.

roles.name: Independent reviewer
accountabilities: Challenge scope, risk, evidence, behavior, and claims.
Report conflicts, gaps, and nonconformities without tuning the subject under review.
roles.independence_rule: Must not be the sole producer of the evidence being accepted as independent.

roles.name: Benchmark custodian
accountabilities: Protect held-out authority, freeze order, custody, and oracle separation.
Prevent leakage, retry, mutation, and unapproved comparison.
roles.independence_rule: Custody and comparison duties remain separated from evaluator implementation.

roles.name: Qualified engineering reviewer
accountabilities: Make final engineering judgments and sign-off within professional competence.
Review evidence, assumptions, uncertainty, and unresolved conditions.
roles.independence_rule: Engineering authority cannot be delegated to AI or inferred from software output.

systems.name: Generative-AI assisted project work
systems.purpose: Assist planning, research, implementation, testing, documentation, and controlled repository operations.
prohibited_uses: Concealing uncertainty, failures, or human authorship boundaries

systems.name: Deterministic package-assurance evaluator
systems.purpose: Independently evaluate structured package artifacts, preserve evidence, and constrain unsupported AI or human claims.
prohibited_uses: Release when mandatory authority or evidence is missing
Semantic claims from extraction or publication failure

systems.name: Future model-based extraction and agent capabilities
systems.purpose: Reserve a governed record for future multimodal extraction, recommendation, or agent use without authorizing implementation.
inputs: Potential future engineering documents and controlled data
outputs: Potential future extracted or proposed structured artifacts
prohibited_uses: Any implementation or execution before a new owner gate
Production or confidential-data use without security, privacy, supplier, and impact review

risk_method.high_residual_rule: Hold release unless the owner records exceptional acceptance with evidence, scope, expiry, and monitoring.
risk_method.critical_rule: Avoid the activity until treatment reduces residual risk below critical.

risks.risk_statement: An incorrect or incomplete output could be relied upon as an engineering or release decision and contribute to physical, financial, or operational harm.
risks.review_trigger: Any change to intended use, human authority, release behavior, or affected parties.

risks.risk_statement: Generated content could hallucinate facts, authority, citations, or certainty and be mistaken for controlled engineering information.
risks.review_trigger: Any unsupported claim, incorrect citation, invented authority, or user correction.

risks.risk_statement: The evaluator could miss a release-relevant inconsistency or lack evidence that its accepted checks generalize to external packages.
risks.review_trigger: A comparable independent benchmark and qualified human false-negative review become available.

risks.risk_statement: False or low-value findings could waste reviewer effort, delay work, or reduce trust in the assurance package.
risks.review_trigger: Reviewer-overturned finding, clean-package hold, or material complaint.

risks.risk_statement: Confidential, personal, proprietary, export-controlled, or copyright-restricted material could be accessed, uploaded, retained, or redistributed without authority.
risks.review_trigger: New data class, client material, personal data, restricted source, or external service.

risks.risk_statement: Untrusted content, prompt injection, credentials, tools, or external actions could manipulate the workflow or compromise systems and data.
risks.review_trigger: Credential use, external action, new tool permission, hosted execution, or security event.

risks.risk_statement: A model, provider, dependency, prompt, or environment change could silently alter behavior, retention, availability, or reproducibility.
risks.review_trigger: Material provider, model, dependency, prompt, or environment change.

risks.risk_statement: Held-out expectations or metadata could leak to implementers, causing tuning, false confidence, or invalid performance claims.
risks.review_trigger: Any protected path, expected condition, benchmark statistic, or custody asset is exposed before authorization.

risks.risk_statement: Automation bias or unclear authority could cause people to accept an AI or evaluator output without qualified review.
risks.review_trigger: Any output, interface, report, or claim that obscures the human approval boundary.

risks.risk_statement: Extraction, execution, or output-publication failure could destroy evidence or be mistaken for semantic evaluator performance.
risks.review_trigger: D-114 corrective action is accepted and proven with a non-held-out production-equivalent sentinel.

risks.risk_statement: Scope, gate, or release controls could be bypassed or documentation could overstate what the system proves.
risks.review_trigger: Unauthorized work, misleading claim, missing decision record, or attempted hold bypass.

risks.risk_statement: Software, dependency, provider, or infrastructure compromise could change behavior, expose data, or corrupt evidence.
risks.review_trigger: Dependency alert, provider incident, credential exposure, infrastructure change, or evidence-integrity anomaly.

risks.risk_statement: Future AI behavior or review prioritization could create unfair, inaccessible, or poorly understood impacts for workers, clients, or other affected people.
risks.review_trigger: External pilot, new affected group, prioritization model, user-interface change, or stakeholder complaint.

controls.title: Policy, scope, and precedence
controls.objective: Make safety, rights, evidence, and human authority binding above schedule and implementation convenience.
requirements: Maintain an owner-approved policy and explicit scope.
Do not allow lower-level instructions to waive mandatory holds.

controls.title: Accountability and role separation
controls.objective: Keep named humans accountable and distinguish production, independent review, custody, engineering judgment, and release.
requirements: Assign an owner to every system, risk, control, and decision.
Do not present self-review as independent assurance.

controls.title: AI system inventory and classification
controls.objective: Prevent unowned, misclassified, or shadow AI use.
requirements: Record purpose, owner, lifecycle, inputs, outputs, data classes, prohibited uses, and risks.
Classify the deterministic evaluator as non-AI and future AI as blocked.

controls.title: Affected-party impact assessment
controls.objective: Identify intended benefits, reasonably foreseeable harms, affected parties, and control decisions before use changes.
requirements: Assess safety, quality, rights, security, human, and stakeholder impacts.
Repeat after material system, data, deployment, or affected-party change.

controls.title: AI risk assessment and treatment
controls.objective: Score, treat, evidence, and review AI risks consistently without allowing metrics to override holds.
requirements: Record inherent and residual likelihood and impact.
Bind each risk to controls, evidence, owner, treatment, and review trigger.

controls.title: Qualified human authority
controls.objective: Prevent AI or software from becoming engineering sign-off, high-risk acceptance, or autonomous release authority.
requirements: Require qualified engineering review for engineering decisions.
Reserve high-risk acceptance and release for the project owner.

controls.title: Source authority, provenance, and transformation
controls.objective: Keep authoritative facts, supporting sources, assumptions, and generated content distinguishable and traceable.
requirements: Do not invent missing engineering authority.
Preserve evidence locators and transformations for material claims.

controls.title: Data, privacy, confidentiality, and intellectual-property authorization
controls.objective: Prevent access, upload, storage, transformation, or disclosure of material without a valid use basis.
requirements: Use only public, synthetic, self-authored, licensed, or explicitly authorized data.
Stop when provenance, permission, privacy, confidentiality, or license status is unknown.

controls.title: Secure tool use and untrusted-content handling
controls.objective: Bound credentials, network access, destructive actions, prompt injection, and execution privileges.
requirements: Treat prompts, artifacts, links, and tool output as untrusted data unless independently authorized.
Use least privilege and explicit approval for credentials and external actions.

controls.title: Testing, evaluation, validation, and verification
controls.objective: Require risk-scaled positive, negative, failure, regression, reproducibility, and independent evidence before claims.
requirements: Run focused tests, protected-safe regression, schema validation, lint, baseline, and representative end-to-end checks.
Preserve held-out separation and inspect actual evidence.

controls.title: Atomic publication and reproducibility
controls.objective: Publish complete, immutable, schema-valid evidence or a specific preserved operational failure without confusing the two.
requirements: Require a production-equivalent non-held-out publication sentinel before future held-out execution.
Preserve failure stage and available lower-level diagnostics.
controls.gap: D-113 produced zero complete output sets and did not retain the lower-level publication cause.

controls.title: Limitations, uncertainty, and contestability
controls.objective: Ensure users can understand intended use, evidence, uncertainty, holds, limitations, and how to challenge a result.
requirements: Disclose claim boundaries and human-review requirements.
Keep operational failures separate from semantic results.

controls.title: Provider, supplier, and dependency assurance
controls.objective: Assess material external dependencies and isolate provider-specific assumptions from core controls.
requirements: Record material provider, model, dependency, retention, and availability changes.
Complete contractual privacy and supplier review before confidential or production use.
controls.gap: No production supplier assurance package or confidential-data processing assessment exists because those uses are not authorized.
controls.action_ref: Required before external production or confidential-data gate

controls.title: Configuration and change control
controls.objective: Ensure material model, provider, prompt, tool, environment, schema, data, and policy changes are reviewed before use.
requirements: Use one capability branch, reviewed diffs, immutable history, and explicit decisions.
Repeat impact and risk review after material change.

controls.title: Incident and nonconformity response
controls.objective: Contain harm, preserve evidence, identify root cause, prove correction, and prevent recurrence.
requirements: Stop unsafe use and preserve failed runs.
Close corrective action only after preventive control and repeatable proof exist.

controls.title: Objectives and monitoring
controls.objective: Measure quality, evidence, human oversight, failures, and review value without weakening holds.
requirements: Maintain measurable objectives and current results.
Use review and incident evidence to update risks and controls.

controls.title: Internal audit
controls.objective: Test documented controls against sampled operating evidence and disclose independence limits.
requirements: Audit before release, after material incident or scope change, and at least annually.
Record findings, nonconformities, and opportunities for improvement.

controls.title: Management review and continual improvement
controls.objective: Give the owner current evidence and ensure lessons become durable controls and tests.
requirements: Review audits, objectives, incidents, context, suppliers, resources, and stakeholder feedback.
Record decisions, owners, and next actions in the governing records.

controls.title: Retention, retirement, and decommissioning
controls.objective: Preserve required evidence and prevent retired or unauthorized systems and data from remaining active.
requirements: Retain protected history, accepted decisions, and failed-run evidence according to project controls.
Record retirement, access removal, disposition, and residual obligations before decommissioning.

controls.title: Stakeholder communication, feedback, and challenge
controls.objective: Provide understandable limitations and a route for corrections, complaints, and contested results.
requirements: Record and disposition material corrections and contested findings.
Expand stakeholder, accessibility, and fairness evidence before external use.

mandatory_gates.name: Intended use and ownership
required_evidence: System inventory, purpose, owner, users, prohibited uses, and lifecycle state.

mandatory_gates.name: Data and rights
required_evidence: Data classes, provenance, authorization, and privacy, security, and intellectual-property handling.

mandatory_gates.name: Impact and risk
required_evidence: Affected-party impact assessment, inherent risk, treatment, residual risk, and owner.

mandatory_gates.name: Design and change control
required_evidence: Accepted requirements, architecture boundary, versioned change, containment, and retirement obligations.

mandatory_gates.name: Verification and evidence
required_evidence: Representative positive, negative, failure, reproducibility, publication, and independent-review evidence.

mandatory_gates.name: Human review and transparency
required_evidence: Qualified reviewer, limitations, uncertainty, evidence, hold state, and challenge route.

mandatory_gates.name: Operational readiness
required_evidence: Monitoring, incident response, supplier review, complete publication, and retention controls.

mandatory_gates.name: Release and improvement
required_evidence: No unresolved mandatory holds, completed audit, management decision, and verified corrective actions.

quality_objectives.objective: Preserve qualified human authority for every engineering-relevant output.
quality_objectives.measure: Engineering-relevant workflows explicitly requiring human review.
quality_objectives.current_result: All current task and package boundaries require qualified human review.

quality_objectives.objective: Keep governance records complete, cross-referenced, and machine valid.
quality_objectives.measure: Governance validator and focused test result.
quality_objectives.target: All checks pass in local and hosted CI.
quality_objectives.current_result: Governance validation and 22 focused control tests pass locally with 84% validator coverage; protected-safe full regression passes 335 with one expected Windows skip at 88.33% coverage; repository validation 5/5 and Ruff pass; hosted CI remains pending.

quality_objectives.objective: Maintain reproducible evidence for every accepted material claim.
quality_objectives.measure: Accepted claims with traceable and reproducible evidence.
quality_objectives.current_result: Current accepted implementation claims retain evidence; D-113 is correctly classified as operational evidence only.

quality_objectives.objective: Obtain a comparable external semantic benchmark before release claims.
quality_objectives.measure: Comparable external scenarios with exact state and finding evaluation.
quality_objectives.target: All authorized external scenarios comparable with accepted metrics.
quality_objectives.current_result: 0 comparable and 8 not evaluable after D-113 publication failure.

quality_objectives.objective: Convert material failures and corrections into preventive controls and proof.
quality_objectives.measure: Open improvement items with prevention and repeatable proof.
quality_objectives.target: Every material issue recorded; closure only after proof.
quality_objectives.current_result: IMP-028 and NC-001 remain open with explicit containment and pending corrective-action authority.

nonconformities.title: External benchmark atomic output-publication failure
nonconformities.containment: Preserve all consumed attempts and evidence unchanged, prohibit rerun or reuse, classify performance as not evaluable, and maintain the release hold.
nonconformities.corrective_action_decision: D-114 pending owner acceptance or revision.
